clinical_coherence.notes: 34M right-handed with first-time GTC preceded by deja vu and epigastric rising. History of childhood febrile seizures and family history of epilepsy are appropriate risk factors. Exam is normal inter-ictally with lateral tongue bite. Occupation listed as 'physical therapist' in demographics but 'software developer' in HPI. Sodium is 154 mEq/L (significant hypernatremia), unexplained in an otherwise healthy 34-year-old.

tool_output_consistency.notes: EEG shows left anterior temporal sharp waves with TIRDA - classic for left TLE. MRI shows left hippocampal sclerosis with volumetric asymmetry. ECG is normal. Labs show elevated post-ictal prolactin (38.9) supporting epileptic seizure. Video-EEG captures two habitual seizures with left temporal onset. PET shows concordant left temporal hypometabolism. Neuropsych shows material-specific verbal memory deficit. All modalities are perfectly concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G40.109 correct. Differential appropriately includes PNES, syncope, migraine with aura, and TIA with good distinguishing features. Critical actions cover EEG, MRI, labs, AED initiation, driving counseling, and epilepsy surgery referral. Reasoning about concordance across modalities is accurate.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Textbook left TLE with MTS: classic aura (deja vu + epigastric rising), concordant EEG, MRI, PET, and neuropsych findings. Appropriately straightforward.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 34M with childhood febrile seizures developing TLE in adulthood is the classic natural history of mesial temporal sclerosis. Family history of febrile seizures and epilepsy adds realistic genetic context.

educational_value.notes: Outstanding teaching case covering temporal lobe aura semiology, febrile seizures as MTS risk factor, multimodal concordance (EEG-MRI-PET-neuropsych), surgical candidacy assessment, and the ERSET trial evidence. Extremely high yield.

issues_found: Occupation mismatch: 'physical therapist' in demographics vs 'software developer' in HPI
Sodium 154 mEq/L (hypernatremia) unexplained in a healthy 34-year-old
Lab interpretation references prolactin of 42.5 ng/mL but actual value is 38.9 ng/mL
strengths: Perfect multimodal concordance across EEG, MRI, PET, and neuropsych
Classic febrile seizure to MTS natural history
Excellent inclusion of ERSET trial evidence in literature search
